steps.title: Use parallel line angle relationships
steps.content:
When a transversal intersects two parallel lines, all acute angles are congruent to each other, all obtuse angles are congruent to each other, and any acute angle is supplementary to any adjacent obtuse angle.

Given that one acute angle is $(9x - 650)°$, the obtuse angle must satisfy:
$$(9x - 650) + \text{obtuse} = 180$$
Solving for the obtuse angle:
$$\text{obtuse} = 830 - 9x$$

steps.title: Substitute into the sum equation and solve
steps.content:
We're told that one acute angle plus three obtuse angles equals $(-18x + w)°$:
$$(9x - 650) + 3(830 - 9x) = -18x + w$$
Expanding the left side:
$$9x - 650 + 2490 - 27x = -18x + w$$
Combining like terms:
$$-18x + 1840 = -18x + w$$
Therefore, $w = 1840$.
